incidents.location: In Hillsborough / Mile Marker 162 Heading South
incidents.reason:
The left lane of I-85 NB/I-40 EB  will be closed from approximately MM 160 to MM 162. This closure will be for construction of permanent barrier wall and median foundations of overhead sign assemblies. The left shoulder on I-85 SB/I-40 WB will also be closed in this section. This will be a continuous 45 day closure from Sunday, March 22nd until Wednesday, May 6th.
A crash cushion and PCB will be placed at both locations of sign foundations, with drums lining the lane and shoulder between sign foundation locations. A crash cushion and PCB will also be placed along the outside right shoulder at one of the overhead sign assemblies.

incidents.reason:
The left lane of I-85 NB/I-40 EB  will be closed from approximately MM 160 to MM 162. This closure will be for construction of permanent barrier wall and median foundations of overhead sign assemblies. The left shoulder on I-85 SB/I-40 WB will also be closed in this section. A crash cushion and PCB will be placed at both locations of sign foundations, with drums lining the lane and shoulder between sign foundation locations. A crash cushion and PCB will also be placed along the outside right shoulder at one of the overhead sign assemblies.

incidents.location: In Charlotte / Mile Marker 44 to 46 Both Directions
incidents.reason:
From the City of Charlotte:
Construction starts soon on the I-85 North Bridge project. The project will construct a new two-lane street between Research Drive and J.W. Clay Boulevard in Charlotte's northeast corridor. This new street includes two bridges: one across Doby Creek and the other spanning I-85. The project will also realign the intersection of Research Drive and David Taylor Drive to provide more efficient travel through the intersection.
Both bridges will connect University City's residential, retail and educational centers on the east side of I-85 to University Research Park's business and employment centers on the west side.
The project will provide an alternative route to both the W.T. Harris Boulevard and Mallard Creek Church Road interchanges with I-85.
Weather and unforeseen circumstances occasionally prevent work from being completed as scheduled. When this occurs, city staff and contractors will attempt to resolve the situation as quickly as possible.
Please obey all regulatory and informational signage to ensure everyone's safety.
